# Purpose
The code defines a single global variable, `__version__`, which is set to the string "1.0.1". This variable is typically used to specify the version number of a software module or package. By convention, `__version__` is a common way to indicate the current version of the code, which can be useful for version tracking, compatibility checks, and documentation purposes. The version number follows the semantic versioning format, which includes major, minor, and patch numbers.
